question: A news helicopter is hovering when it sees an airliner flying across its field of view from an 11 o'clock position to a 1 o'clock position in 61 seconds at the same altitude. If the aircraft are 4 SM apart, what is the airliner's airspeed?

You're in Mexico and the fuel truck pulls up to ask how much fuel you need. You know you want 2,500 LBS of Jet A, but he needs to know what it is in Liters. What do you tell him? Assume a gallon of Jet A weighs 6.7 pounds.
The Cherokee you rented has a usable fuel capacity of 36 U.S. gallons. How far can you fly in still air, landing with FAA VFR day fuel reserves? You lean to burn 15.785 kg/hour and cruise at 115 mph.
You're 2 inches out of CG. How much weight do you need to move to station 110 from station 50 to get back into CG? Total aircraft weight is 6850 LBS.
Tuning in the ASOS, you hear current winds of 230° at 17 KTS, gusting to 29 KTS. What is the steady headwind component on runway 18L?

question: You are orbiting a fire at 3 NM and 12,000'. If you make 9 full circles around the fire, how many nautical miles did you travel?

You have decided to upgrade your old aluminum prop to a new composite one! The old prop weighed 62 LBS while the composite model weights 41 LBS. Prior to the swap your aircraft weighed 1475 LBS with a CG of 11.63". If the prop is at -61" what will the new CG fall after the swap?

question: The propeller manufacturer advertises a 17% reduction in takeoff distance. If your takeoff distance was 763' what do you anticipate for your new takeoff distance?

question: You are flying your Glasair on a quick cross country flight at 4,500' MSL, where the temperature is 12°C, and your altimeter set to the local setting of 30.15". Cruise speed is an impressive 245 KIAS. The cruise portion of your flight is 79 NM and takes only 16 minutes. Following your GPS course of 052° true, you see that you are flying with a 4° crab to the left. What were the true winds you experienced in cruise?

You are 59 NM into your 153 NM flight when your passenger starts having chest pains. Knowing it will take you 3 minutes to complete the calculations, would it be faster to turn back to your origin or continue to the destination? You are cruising at 8,500' (ISA) and 117 KIAS. The winds aloft at your altitude are 290° at 22 KTS and you are flying a true course of 131°.
choice_a: It is a little over 2 minutes faster to continue to the destination.
choice_b: It is about 2 and a half minutes faster to turn back.
choice_c: It is just under 4 minutes faster to continue to the destination.
choice_d: It is about 5 and a half minutes faster to turn back.
correct_choice: It is a little over 2 minutes faster to continue to the destination.

question: Each wing of your 1959 Cessna 182 is 16 feet long and 3 feet wide. If you take off at 2600 LBS and make an immediate 50° bank turn, what is the wing loading in the turn?

The descent angle on your instrument approach into Aspen is a hair-raising 6.6°. You are approaching at 112 KIAS on a true course of 151°. You have a direct headwind of 23 knots and begin your descent at 11,700' with an OAT of -2°C. If the altimeter setting is 30.41", what descent rate will you use? Assume constant TAS in descent.

With you, a passenger, and full fuel, your aircraft weighs in at 902 kilograms with a CG of 43.7". How much baggage can you carry in your cargo compartment (73.2" aft) without exceeding the CG limit of 45"?

question: As you start up your Cessna, a grasshopper is holding onto the end of the propeller blade for dear life. At an idle speed of 800 RPM, how fast is the grasshopper traveling if your propeller diameter is 54"?

You are planning a short trip in your vintage plane. Your route is 48 NM on a true course of 317°. True winds aloft are 150° at 18 KTS at your cruising altitude of 3,500' (ISA). Your plane cruises at 98 mph indicated while sipping only 5.5 gallons per hour of fuel. If you top off the single 42 liter tank, how much reserve fuel will you land with?

You are breaking in a new engine, and you want to cruise at 80% power for as long as possible. At that power setting, your plane cruises at 137 KTAS and burns 12.4 GPH. You are going to follow the highway which runs exactly west from your home airport, and today's winds aloft are 220° at 47 KTS. You depart with the tanks topped off at 62 gallons. How far can you fly before turning back if you want to land with 30 minutes of reserve fuel?

You join a 35 DME arc at the 040 radial while flying 236 KIAS. You are flying at 8,000' (ISA -7°C), and the altimeter setting is 29.67". Assuming calm winds, how long will it take to fly 15° of the arc?

You are cruising at FL370 at ISA+4°C, and your speed is M0.79. With 239 NM remaining, ATC instructs you to reduce your speed to M0.75. Disregarding the descent, how much time did this add to the flight?
1437 NM equals how many SM?
337 hours equals how many weeks?
Traveling 120 feet in 4.7 seconds is equivalent to how many miles per hour?
19 feet equals how many centimeters?

Considering this, how much avgas burn does this system replace if the manual states that 33.7 kWh equals one gallon of avgas? Hint: 1 kWh = 1 kW of power used in one hour
